facts_disclosed: Feels trapped in Abu Dhabi, wants international experience

turns.content: hey. you up? lol I know you're an AI you're always up. I just need to vent for a second
I'm here! Go ahead — what's on your mind?

idk it's 2am and I just got off instagram and I'm in my feelings. my friend Nour posted about her studio in London, she's doing this incredible adaptive reuse project. and my friend from AUS is in Copenhagen doing social housing. another one just moved to New York for a fellowship. and I'm just... here. wallah sometimes I feel so stuck

That sounds like a rough scroll. Social media compresses everyone else's highlights into a single feed that makes it feel like the world is accelerating except for you. But those are all very different people in very different situations — it just looks like one unified wave from your screen.

What specifically is getting to you? Is it the work they're doing, or the fact that they're somewhere else doing it?

both tbh. I want to see how firms work in places where sustainability isn't just a luxury villa add-on. like in Scandinavia it's embedded in the building code. in London there's this whole retrofit culture. I want to absorb that and bring it back. but also just... I'm 23 and I've never lived anywhere else? I was born here, went to school here, got my job here. I've never been the new person anywhere. I want to experience something different ngl

There's a real difference between choosing to stay somewhere because it's where you want to be, and feeling like you've never had the chance to find out what else might fit. It sounds like you're feeling more of the second one right now.

What's keeping you from exploring it? Is it practical — finances, visa — or more personal?

it's my parents. they'd never say I can't leave but I'm the only one nearby. my brother is in Canada, my sister is married in Jeddah. I'm the one who goes to mama's for Friday lunch, who drives baba to his appointments. if I leave who does that? I can't just be like bye I'm going to London to find myself lol. and wallah mama already cries every time she facetimes my brother in Montreal. I can't do that to her

That's a real responsibility, and the fact that you take it seriously says a lot about you. But your mother raised three children — one is in Canada, one is in Saudi Arabia. She's already navigated letting go twice. That doesn't mean it was painless, but it means she's capable of it. Sometimes parents' sadness and pride coexist.

You don't have to decide anything tonight at 2am, though.

ngl you're right I should not be making life decisions at this hour. I just get in my head at night. like during the day I'm busy at work and it's fine and then the sun goes down and suddenly I'm like what am I doing with my life?? am I going to be drawing villa elevations in Abu Dhabi until I'm 40?? I feel like I'm watching everyone's career explode from my phone screen

The late-night spiral is real. Everything feels more permanent and more urgent at 2am. During the day your life has momentum and context — at night it flattens into a single question.

Here's what I'd suggest — not tonight, but soon: start researching what an international move would actually look like. Not committing, just informing. What firms interest you? What visa would you need? Right now the idea is this big amorphous longing, and that's the hardest version of it. Making it concrete would tell you whether it's a real goal or a 2am mood.

that's actually smart. like just look at what's out there without the pressure of deciding. okay. yalla I need to sleep before I spiral more. thanks for letting me dump all of that on you wallah I feel lighter already
Anytime. And for what it's worth, wanting more for yourself isn't ungrateful — it's ambitious. Go get some sleep. The research can wait for daylight. Goodnight!
goodnight. and thanks for real. I needed this
Sleep well. Tomorrow's a new day to figure things out — no rush.
